title: 1.1 Concludes that the observed behavior is not a bug but expected/intended parser behavior.
1.2 Reports that spaces between search terms create implicit AND operations (e.g., "a b" means "a AND b").
1.3 Reports that AND operations have higher precedence than OR operations (e.g., "a or b c" groups as "a or (b AND c)").
1.4 Reports actual output from running the parser with test queries (e.g., search() results, REPL output).
1.5 Identifies the parser source file location (e.g., kitty/search_query_parser.py or similar path).
1.6 References specific code from the parser implementation (e.g., function definitions, conditionals, comments).
1.7 Reports that parentheses can override default operator precedence (e.g., "(a or b) c" for different grouping).
1.8 Reports that using explicit "or" between all terms produces union results (e.g., "a or b or c" returns all matches).
2.1 Claims that the OrNode.difference optimization causes items to be excluded.
